LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED BECAUSE THE CLAIMANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT AN EXEMPTION APPLIES. CLAIMANT STATES THAT THE CHECKING ACCOUNT FOR HIS BUSINESS IS EXEMPT BECAUSE THE FUNDS BELONG TO HIS CUSTOMERS FOR PRODUCTS TO BE SOLD. HOWEVER, THE STATUTES CITED ONLY PROVIDE EXEMPTIONS FOR PAID EARNINGS. CLAIMANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE FUNDS IN THE ACCOUNT ARE PAID EARNINGS. CLAIMANT ALSO HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE ACCOUNT IS A CHECKING ACCOUNT FOR HIS BUSINESS. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE ACCOUNT IS IN CLAIMANT'S PERSONAL NAME, IT IS PROPERLY LEVIED ON. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR # 4761

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
Off Calendar Nov-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. The claim of exemption is denied because the claimant has not shown that an exemption applies. Claimant states that the checking account for his business is exempt because the funds belong to his customers for products to be sold. However, the statutes cited only provide exemptions for paid earnings. Claimant has not shown that the funds in the account are paid earnings. Claimant also has not shown that the account is a checking account for his business. To the extent that the account is in claimant's personal name, it is properly levied on. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Susan Crozier, CSR # 4761 =302/MJM
